text:
# Part 27 — Hearings and Directions Appointments

## PART 27 E+W HEARINGS AND DIRECTIONS APPOINTMENTS


### Application of this Part E+W


27.1. This Part is subject to any enactment, any provision in these rules or a practice direction. (Rule 27.4(7) makes additional provision in relation to requirements to stay proceedings where the respondent does not appear and a relevant European regulation or international convention applies)


### Reasons for a decision [ F1 : proceedings before a lay justice or justices ] E+W


27.2. —(1) This rule applies to proceedings [ F2 in the family court before a lay justice or justices ] .


(2) After a hearing, the court will make its decision as soon as is practicable.


(3) The court must give written reasons for its decision.


(4) Paragraph s (5) and (6) apply where the functions of the court are being performed by—


(5) The [ F3 justices’ legal adviser ] must, before the court makes an order or refuses an application or request, make notes of—


(6) The [ F3 justices’ legal adviser ] must make a written record of the reasons for the court's decision.


(7) When making an order or refusing an application, the court, or one of the [ F6 lay ] justices constituting the court by which the decision is made, will announce its decision and—


(8) Subject to any other rule or practice direction, the court officer will supply a copy of the order and the reasons for the court's decision to the persons referred to in paragraph (9)—


(9) The persons referred to in paragraph (8) are—


F7 (10) . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .


Textual Amendments


F1 Words in rule 27.2 heading substituted (22.4.2014) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2014 (S.I. 2014/667) , rules 1 , 19(a) (with rule 45 )
